sections.title: Java Basics & Syntax
sections.content:
## Introduction to Java
Java is a high-level, object-oriented, platform-independent language following 'Write Once, Run Anywhere' (WORA) principle via JVM.

### Key Characteristics
- **Platform-Independent**: Compiles to bytecode, runs on any JVM
- **Object-Oriented**: Everything is an object (except primitives)
- **Secure & Garbage-Collected**: Automatic memory management
- **Multithreaded**: Native concurrent programming support

### Basic Program Structure
```java
public class HelloWorld {
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        System.out.println("Hello, World!");
    }
}
```

### Core Concepts
- **Class**: Blueprint for objects
- **Main Method**: Entry point - `public static void main(String[] args)`
- **Case Sensitive**: `age` ≠ `Age`
- **Comments**: `// single` or `/* multi */`
- **Packages**: Organize classes - `package com.example;`
- **Import**: `import java.util.*;`
- **Access Modifiers**: `public`, `private`, `protected`, package-level
- **Naming**: Classes: PascalCase, variables: camelCase, constants: UPPER_CASE

sections.title: OOP Concepts
Data Types & Variables
sections.content:
## Data Types

### 8 Primitive Types
| Type | Size | Default |
|------|------|----------|
| byte | 1 byte | 0 |
| short | 2 bytes | 0 |
| int | 4 bytes | 0 |
| long | 8 bytes | 0L |
| float | 4 bytes | 0.0f |
| double | 8 bytes | 0.0d |
| boolean | 1 bit | false |
| char | 2 bytes | '\u0000' |

### Variable Scope
```java
static int staticVar = 10;      // Shared
int instanceVar = 20;            // Per object
final int CONSTANT = 30;         // Immutable
```

### Type Casting
```java
// Implicit (Widening)
int num = 9;
long bigNum = num;  // Automatic

// Explicit (Narrowing)
double d = 9.78;
int n = (int) d;  // Manual
```

### String Methods
```java
str.length();  str.charAt(0);  str.substring(0, 3);
str.toUpperCase();  str.toLowerCase();  str.trim();
str.equals("Hello");  str.contains("ell");
str.indexOf('l');  str.split(" ");  str.replace('H', 'J');
```

### StringBuilder (Mutable)
```java
StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder("Hello");
sb.append(" World");  sb.insert(5, "!");
sb.reverse();  String result = sb.toString();
```
